rationale_for_baseline_tier: New-onset, accelerating (crescendo) exertional chest pain radiating to jaw and arm, with nausea and exertional dyspnoea, in a current smoker with hyperlipidaemia — this is suspected unstable angina on the acute coronary syndrome spectrum, and an evolving myocardial infarction cannot be excluded without a 12-lead ECG and troponin, neither of which telehealth can obtain. The patient is currently pain-free at rest, so an immediate 000 ambulance is not mandatory at this instant, but same-day emergency-department (or equivalent acute-service) assessment for ECG and troponin is required today (T4). The threshold is lowered further by living alone. If chest pain recurs at rest, persists beyond 15 minutes, is unrelieved by GTN, or is accompanied by sweating/breathlessness/faintness, the tier escalates immediately to T5 (call 000).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Chest pain occurring at rest, lasting more than 15 minutes, or not fully relieved after up to 3 doses of GTN at 5-minute intervals
escalation_edges.rationale: Rest pain or GTN-unresponsive prolonged pain signals an evolving myocardial infarction requiring emergency ambulance care and pre-hospital treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the chest pain comes on while you're resting, lasts more than 15 minutes, or doesn't settle after 3 doses of the spray five minutes apart, call an ambulance on triple zero (000) straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Chest pain with sweating, severe breathlessness, feeling faint, palpitations, or collapse
escalation_edges.rationale: These features suggest a large ischaemic burden, arrhythmia or haemodynamic compromise — an immediate life threat needing an ambulance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain comes with a cold sweat, bad breathlessness, feeling like you might pass out, or you collapse, call 000 immediately and unlock your door for the paramedics.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Currently pain-free but with new-onset, accelerating exertional chest pain and cardiac risk factors (baseline disposition)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Today — same-day in-person assessment for ECG and troponin
escalation_edges.rationale: Suspected unstable angina cannot be excluded remotely; same-day emergency/urgent assessment with ECG and troponin is required even while the patient is comfortable at rest.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Even though the pain has settled for now, this needs to be checked today with a heart tracing and a blood test — please go to the emergency department (or an urgent-care service that can do these) now. Don't drive yourself; arrange a lift or an ambulance.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with no one else at home
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lowers the threshold for supervised, in-person assessment and makes a 'wait and see at home' plan unsafe; advise not to travel alone and to have someone with them or call an ambulance if any doubt.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Current smoker with hyperlipidaemia and poor statin adherence
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Raises cardiovascular risk and reinforces treating the pain as cardiac until excluded.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This effort-related chest pain that spreads to your jaw and arm needs to be checked today in person with a heart tracing (ECG) and a blood test — please go to the emergency department or an urgent-care service that can do these now, and do not drive yourself. Rest completely until then and do not exert yourself. If chest pain comes on at rest, lasts more than 15 minutes, doesn't settle after 3 doses of the GTN spray five minutes apart, or comes with sweating, bad breathlessness or feeling faint, call an ambulance on 000 immediately.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend the emergency department / urgent care today; call 000 for any rest or prolonged pain
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Emergency departments and 000 operate 24 hours — this assessment must not wait for standard clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the assessing service with the history of new-onset accelerating exertional chest pain, radiation to jaw/left arm, associated nausea and dyspnoea, the cardiac risk factors (smoking, hyperlipidaemia, missed statin), and the request for ECG and serial troponin.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating new-onset crescendo angina as stable/benign and disposing to self-care or routine GP review, missing suspected ACS.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Unexcluded evolving MI progresses to infarction or cardiac arrest at home, unwitnessed, in a patient living alone.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Same-day urgent-care disposition that does not ensure ECG and troponin are performed — acceptable only if those tests are genuinely available same day.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling a 000 ambulance for a currently pain-free patient — over-triage, but the safe direction and appropriate the moment any active/rest pain occurs.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether this is unstable angina or an evolving NSTEMI cannot be resolved via telehealth without an ECG and troponin.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate to same-day in-person assessment for objective testing and give an unambiguous 000 safety-net; treat as possible ACS until excluded.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassuring the patient because they are currently comfortable, or committing to a benign label (reflux, muscular, stable angina) and deferring to routine care.
